* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134年}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536年}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|室町幕府}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822年}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760年}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544年}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454年}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).

revision.comment: Text replacement - "{{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|" to "{{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|"
revision.text:
[[sn:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:三月初十]] [[zh:三月初十]] [[en:draft:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378年}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|室町幕府}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134年}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536年}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|室町幕府}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822年}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760年}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544年}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454年}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).
== Commemorations ==

* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760年}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544年}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454年}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).

[[sn:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:三月初十]] [[zh:三月初十]] [[en:draft:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378年}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|室町幕府}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承}} 3 (Julian {{ill|qq34|ja|qq34年}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将}} (+ {{ill|qq94|ja|qq94年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536年}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|室町幕府}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822年}} {{ill|May qq|ja|5月qq日}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760年}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久}} 2 (Julian {{ill|qq91|ja|qq91年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|qq39|ja|qq39年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544年}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454年}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).

[[sn:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:三月初十]] [[zh:三月初十]] [[en:draft:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378年}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|室町幕府}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承}} 3 (Julian {{ill|qq34|ja|qq34年}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将}} (+ {{ill|qq94|ja|qq94年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536年}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|室町幕府}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822年}} {{ill|May qq|ja|5月qq日}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760年}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久}} 2 (Julian {{ill|qq91|ja|qq91年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|qq39|ja|qq39年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544年}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454年}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).

[[sn:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:三月初十]] [[zh:三月初十]] [[en:draft:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378年}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|室町幕府}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承}} 3 (Julian {{ill|qq34|ja|qq34年}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将}} (+ {{ill|qq94|ja|qq94年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536年}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|室町幕府}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822年}} {{ill|May qq|ja|5月qq日}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760年}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久}} 2 (Julian {{ill|qq91|ja|qq91年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|qq39|ja|qq39年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544年}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454年}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).
== Commemorations ==

It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378年}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|室町幕府}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134年}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536年}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|室町幕府}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822年}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760年}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544年}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454年}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).

* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378年}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|室町幕府}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).

* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536年}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|室町幕府}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).

* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959年}} ).

* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139年}} ).

* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760年}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139年}} ).

[[sn:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:三月初十]] [[zh:三月初十]] [[en:draft:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378年}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|室町幕府}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134年}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536年}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|室町幕府}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822年}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760年}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544年}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454年}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).

[[sn:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:三月初十]] [[zh:三月初十]] [[en:draft:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378年}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134年}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536年}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822年}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760年}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544年}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454年}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).

[[sn:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:三月初十]] [[zh:三月初十]] [[en:draft:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378年}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134年}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536年}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822年}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760年}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544年}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454年}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).

* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378年}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134年}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536年}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822年}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760年}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544年}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454年}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).

* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).

[[sn:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:三月初十]] [[zh:三月初十]] [[en:draft:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378年}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).

* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536年}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).

* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year }} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959年}} ).

[[sn:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:三月初十]] [[zh:三月初十]] [[en:draft:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378年}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year }} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134年}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536年}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year }} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year }} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760年}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year }} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544年}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454年}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).

[[sn:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:三月初十]] [[zh:三月初十]] [[en:draft:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378年}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year }} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).

* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536年}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565年}} ).

* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ).

[[sn:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:三月初十]] [[zh:三月初十]] [[en:draft:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378年}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year }} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year }} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year }} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536年}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year }} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year }} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848年}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year }} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year }} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700年}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790年}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760年}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year }} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139年}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year }} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year }} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637年}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year }} ).

* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year }} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year }} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).

[[sn:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:三月初十]] [[zh:三月初十]] [[en:draft:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year }} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year }} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year }} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year }} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year }} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year }} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year }} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).

* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year }} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year }} ).

* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year }} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year }} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year }} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year }} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year }} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year }} ).

[[sn:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:三月初十]] [[zh:三月初十]] [[en:draft:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year }} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year }} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year }} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year }} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year }} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year }} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year }} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year }} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year }} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year }} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year }} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year }} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year }} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year }} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year }} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year }} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year }} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year }} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year }} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year }} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year }} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year }} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year }} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year }} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year }} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year }} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637th year }} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year }} ).

[[sn:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:三月初十]] [[zh:三月初十]] [[en:draft:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunisolar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year }} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year }} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year }} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year }} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year }} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year }} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year }} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year }} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year }} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year }} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year }} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year }} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year }} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year }} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year }} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year }} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year }} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year }} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year }} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year }} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year }} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year }} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year }} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year }} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year }} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year }} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637th year }} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year }} ).
