revision.text:
{{Lunisolar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).

* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).

* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Japanese Traditional Lunisolar Months" to "Japanese Lunisolar Calendar"
revision.text:
{{Lunisolar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Commemorations ==
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|9|3|11|2|10|4|3|0310}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} {{draft categories| }}

* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).

revision.text:
{{Lunisolar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Commemorations ==

It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).

revision.text:
{{Lunisolar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).

* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).

{{DEFAULTSORT:March 10 (Lunisolar)}}

{{Lunisolar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).

[[de:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[sn:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:三月初十]] [[en:draft:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ja:和銅3rd year の出来事]] [[sn:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[en:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[sn:arch 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ar:March 10 (Lunisolar)]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:March 10 (Lunisolar)}}

{{Lunisolar March}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637th year}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year}} ).

* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637th year}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year}} ).

* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|qq=}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|qq=}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久|qq=}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year|qq=}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|qq=}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|qq=}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|qq=}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永|qq=}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純|qq=}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=}} ).

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Lunar Calendar Days" to "Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar"
revision.text:
{{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|qq=}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|qq=}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|qq=}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|qq=}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|qq=}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|qq=}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|qq=}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|qq=}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|qq=}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|qq=}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|qq=}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|qq=}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承|qq=}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定|qq=}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year|qq=}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝|qq=}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year|qq=}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|qq=}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|qq=}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|qq=}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|qq=}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|qq=}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|qq=}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year|qq=}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|qq=}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|qq=}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久|qq=}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year|qq=}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|qq=}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|qq=}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|qq=}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永|qq=}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純|qq=}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=}} ).
== Commemorations ==

{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|qq=}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|qq=}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|qq=}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|qq=}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|qq=}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|qq=}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|qq=}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|qq=}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|qq=}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|qq=}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|qq=}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|qq=}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承|qq=}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定|qq=}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year|qq=}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝|qq=}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year|qq=}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|qq=}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|qq=}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|qq=}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|qq=}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|qq=}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|qq=}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year|qq=}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|qq=}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|qq=}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久|qq=}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year|qq=}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|qq=}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|qq=}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|qq=}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永|qq=}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純|qq=}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=}} ).
