{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|qq=}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|qq=}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|qq=}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|qq=}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|qq=}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|qq=}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|qq=}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|qq=}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|qq=}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|qq=}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|qq=}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|qq=}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承|qq=}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定|qq=}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year|qq=}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝|qq=}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year|qq=}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|qq=}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|qq=}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|qq=}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|qq=}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|qq=}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|qq=}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year|qq=}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|qq=}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|qq=}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久|qq=}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year|qq=}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|qq=}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|qq=}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|qq=}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永|qq=}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純|qq=}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=}} ).

revision.comment: 33 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:3月10日 (旧暦) up to rev 83371459
revision.text:
{{旧暦3月}} '''旧暦3月10日'''は[[3月 (旧暦)|旧暦3月]]の10日目である。六曜は[[六曜#赤口|赤口]]である。
== できごと == * [[文武天皇]]4年（[[ユリウス暦]][[700年]][[4月3日]]） - 法相宗の僧・道昭が[[火葬]]される。記録上日本初の火葬 * [[和銅]]3年（ユリウス暦[[710年]][[4月13日]]） - [[元明天皇]]が[[藤原京]]から[[平城京]]に遷都 * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]8年（閏3月）（ユリウス暦[[866年]][[4月28日]]） - [[応天門の変]]。応天門が炎上。放火の犯人として[[大伴氏]]の末裔・伴善男が流刑にされ、大伴氏が歴史の表舞台から消える * [[永和 (日本)|永和]]4年/[[天授 (日本)|天授]]4年（ユリウス暦[[1378年]][[4月7日]]） - [[足利義満]]が、京都室町に造営した室町殿（花の御所）に幕府を移転 * [[道光]]19年（[[グレゴリオ暦]][[1839年]][[4月23日]]） - [[清]]朝の官僚が[[阿片]]取締りの為に広東へ到着。没収・廃棄など強攻策を推進 * [[明治]]2年（グレゴリオ暦[[1869年]][[4月21日]]） - [[東京市]]で[[町名主]]制を廃止。238人の名主を罷免 * 明治5年（グレゴリオ暦[[1872年]][[4月17日]]） - [[文部省]]博物局（現在の[[東京国立博物館]]）が[[湯島聖堂]]の大成殿を「文部省博物館」とし、[[博覧会]]を開催（[[湯島聖堂博覧会]]）
== 誕生日 == * [[長承]]3年（ユリウス暦[[1134年]][[4月6日]]） - [[安田義定]]、 [[武将]]（+ [[1194年]]） * [[天文 (元号)|天文]]5年（ユリウス暦[[1536年]][[3月31日]]） - [[足利義輝]]、 [[室町幕府]]13代[[征夷大将軍|将軍]]（+ [[1565年]]） * [[文政]]5年（グレゴリオ暦[[1822年]][[5月11日]]） - [[栗本鋤雲]]、[[幕臣]]・[[ジャーナリスト]]（+ [[1897年]]） * [[嘉永]]元年（グレゴリオ暦[[1848年]][[4月13日]]） - [[浅野総一郎|初代浅野総一郎]]、[[実業家]]・浅野財閥創始（+ [[1930年]]） * [[慶応]]2年（グレゴリオ暦[[1866年]][[4月24日]]） - [[石井菊次郎]]、[[外交官]]（+ [[1945年]]） * [[明治]]4年（グレゴリオ暦[[1871年]][[4月29日]]） - [[吉岡弥生]]、医師・東京女医学校（現在の[[東京女子医科大学]]）創立 （+ [[1959年]]）
== 忌日 == * [[文武天皇]]4年（ユリウス暦[[700年]][[4月3日]]） - [[道昭]]、僧 * [[延暦]]9年閏3月（ユリウス暦[[790年]][[4月28日]]） - [[藤原乙牟漏]]、[[桓武天皇]]の皇后（* [[760年]]） * [[建久]]2年（ユリウス暦[[1191年]][[4月5日]]） - [[藤原長方]]、権中納言・家集『按納言長方集』、日記『禅中記』（* [[1139年]]） * [[天文 (元号)|天文]]15年（ユリウス暦[[1544年]][[4月10日]]） - [[龍造寺家兼]]、[[九州]]の[[戦国大名]]（*[[1454年]]） * [[寛永]]14年（[[1637年]][[4月5日]]） - [[本多正純]]、[[江戸]]時代初期の[[大名]]（* [[1565年]]）
== 記念日・年中行事 ==
== 関連項目 == {{旧暦360日|3|9|3|11|2|10|4|3|0310}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[3月10日 (旧暦)]] to [[March 10 (Lunisolar)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{旧暦3月}} '''旧暦3月10日'''は[[3月 (旧暦)|旧暦3月]]の10日目である。六曜は[[六曜#赤口|赤口]]である。
== できごと == * [[文武天皇]]4年（[[ユリウス暦]][[700年]][[4月3日]]） - 法相宗の僧・道昭が[[火葬]]される。記録上日本初の火葬 * [[和銅]]3年（ユリウス暦[[710年]][[4月13日]]） - [[元明天皇]]が[[藤原京]]から[[平城京]]に遷都 * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]8年（閏3月）（ユリウス暦[[866年]][[4月28日]]） - [[応天門の変]]。応天門が炎上。放火の犯人として[[大伴氏]]の末裔・伴善男が流刑にされ、大伴氏が歴史の表舞台から消える * [[永和 (日本)|永和]]4年/[[天授 (日本)|天授]]4年（ユリウス暦[[1378年]][[4月7日]]） - [[足利義満]]が、京都室町に造営した室町殿（花の御所）に幕府を移転 * [[道光]]19年（[[グレゴリオ暦]][[1839年]][[4月23日]]） - [[清]]朝の官僚が[[阿片]]取締りの為に広東へ到着。没収・廃棄など強攻策を推進 * [[明治]]2年（グレゴリオ暦[[1869年]][[4月21日]]） - [[東京市]]で[[町名主]]制を廃止。238人の名主を罷免 * 明治5年（グレゴリオ暦[[1872年]][[4月17日]]） - [[文部省]]博物局（現在の[[東京国立博物館]]）が[[湯島聖堂]]の大成殿を「文部省博物館」とし、[[博覧会]]を開催（[[湯島聖堂博覧会]]）
== 誕生日 == * [[長承]]3年（ユリウス暦[[1134年]][[4月6日]]） - [[安田義定]]、 [[武将]]（+ [[1194年]]） * [[天文 (元号)|天文]]5年（ユリウス暦[[1536年]][[3月31日]]） - [[足利義輝]]、 [[室町幕府]]13代[[征夷大将軍|将軍]]（+ [[1565年]]） * [[文政]]5年（グレゴリオ暦[[1822年]][[5月11日]]） - [[栗本鋤雲]]、[[幕臣]]・[[ジャーナリスト]]（+ [[1897年]]） * [[嘉永]]元年（グレゴリオ暦[[1848年]][[4月13日]]） - [[浅野総一郎|初代浅野総一郎]]、[[実業家]]・浅野財閥創始（+ [[1930年]]） * [[慶応]]2年（グレゴリオ暦[[1866年]][[4月24日]]） - [[石井菊次郎]]、[[外交官]]（+ [[1945年]]） * [[明治]]4年（グレゴリオ暦[[1871年]][[4月29日]]） - [[吉岡弥生]]、医師・東京女医学校（現在の[[東京女子医科大学]]）創立 （+ [[1959年]]）
== 忌日 == * [[文武天皇]]4年（ユリウス暦[[700年]][[4月3日]]） - [[道昭]]、僧 * [[延暦]]9年閏3月（ユリウス暦[[790年]][[4月28日]]） - [[藤原乙牟漏]]、[[桓武天皇]]の皇后（* [[760年]]） * [[建久]]2年（ユリウス暦[[1191年]][[4月5日]]） - [[藤原長方]]、権中納言・家集『按納言長方集』、日記『禅中記』（* [[1139年]]） * [[天文 (元号)|天文]]15年（ユリウス暦[[1544年]][[4月10日]]） - [[龍造寺家兼]]、[[九州]]の[[戦国大名]]（*[[1454年]]） * [[寛永]]14年（[[1637年]][[4月5日]]） - [[本多正純]]、[[江戸]]時代初期の[[大名]]（* [[1565年]]）
== 記念日・年中行事 ==
== 関連項目 == {{旧暦360日|3|9|3|11|2|10|4|3|0310}}

{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|qq=}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|qq=}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|qq=}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|qq=}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|qq=}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|qq=}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|qq=}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|qq=}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|qq=}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|qq=}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|qq=}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|qq=}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承|qq=}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定|qq=}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year|qq=}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝|qq=}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year|qq=}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|qq=}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|qq=}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|qq=}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|qq=}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|qq=}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|qq=}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year|qq=}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|qq=}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|qq=}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久|qq=}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year|qq=}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|qq=}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|qq=}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|qq=}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永|qq=}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純|qq=}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=}} ).
== Commemorations ==
== See Also ==

* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|qq=}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|qq=}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.

== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承|qq=}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定|qq=}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year|qq=}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝|qq=}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year|qq=}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|qq=}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|qq=}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|qq=}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|qq=}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|qq=}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|qq=}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year|qq=}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|qq=}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|qq=}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久|qq=}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year|qq=}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|qq=}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|qq=}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|qq=}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永|qq=}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637th year|qq=}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純|qq=}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=}} ).

{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|12=draft}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=|12=draft}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|qq=|12=draft}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=|12=draft}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|qq=|12=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=|12=draft}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=|12=draft}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|qq=|12=draft}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|qq=|12=draft}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|qq=|12=draft}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|qq=|12=draft}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=|12=draft}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=|12=draft}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|qq=|12=draft}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=|12=draft}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|12=draft}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=|12=draft}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=|12=draft}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=|12=draft}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=|12=draft}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|qq=|12=draft}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=|12=draft}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|qq=|12=draft}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=|12=draft}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|qq=|12=draft}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|qq=|12=draft}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承|qq=|12=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定|qq=|12=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|qq=|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year|qq=|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|12=draft}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝|qq=|12=draft}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|12=draft}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|qq=|12=draft}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|qq=|12=draft}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|qq=|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|qq=|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|qq=|12=draft}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|qq=|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|qq=|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|qq=|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|qq=|12=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|qq=|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|qq=|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|12=draft}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|qq=|12=draft}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|qq=|12=draft}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year|qq=|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=|12=draft}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|qq=|12=draft}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|qq=|12=draft}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=|12=draft}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year|qq=|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久|qq=|12=draft}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|qq=|12=draft}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year|qq=|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|12=draft}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|qq=|12=draft}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|qq=|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year|qq=|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永|qq=|12=draft}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純|qq=|12=draft}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=|12=draft}} ).

* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=|12=draft|12=draft}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|qq=|12=draft|12=draft}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year|qq=|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|qq=|12=draft|12=draft}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year|qq=|12=draft|12=draft}} ).

{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).

* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).

* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
== Commemorations ==
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|9|3|11|2|10|4|3|0310|qq=}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] to [[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
== Commemorations ==
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|9|3|11|2|10|4|3|0310|qq=}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}

* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
== Commemorations ==

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Lunisolar March]] to [[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191st year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ).

Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|700|ja|700th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/700|qq=}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 3|qq=}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōshō|qq=}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō (era)|qq=}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/710|qq=}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 13|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara-kyō|qq=}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan (Japan)|qq=}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/866|qq=}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 28|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtenmon Incident|qq=}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtenmon Gate|qq=}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ban no Yoshio|qq=}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo clan|qq=}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|qq=}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenju (Japan)|qq=}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1378|qq=}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 7|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|qq=}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|qq=}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|qq=}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1839|qq=}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 23|qq=}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|qq=}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|qq=}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji (era)|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1869|qq=}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 21|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo City|qq=}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|qq=}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872|qq=}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 17|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Education (Japan)|qq=}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|qq=}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yushima Seidō|qq=}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yushima Seidō Exposition|qq=}} ).

* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshō (era)|qq=}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1134|qq=}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 6|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasuda Yoshisada|qq=}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1194|qq=}} ).

* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei (era)|qq=}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822nd year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822|qq=}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 11|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikoma Jōun|qq=}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bakufu|qq=}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/journalist|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1897|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaei (era)|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1848|qq=}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 13|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asano Sōichirō|qq=}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asano zaibatsu|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1930|qq=}} ).

* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|qq=}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenju (Japan)|qq=}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1378|qq=}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 7|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|qq=}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|qq=}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|qq=}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1839|qq=}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 23|qq=}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|qq=}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|qq=}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji (era)|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1869|qq=}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 21|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo City|qq=}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|qq=}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872|qq=}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 17|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Education (Japan)|qq=}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|qq=}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yushima Seidō|qq=}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yushima Seidō Exposition|qq=}} ).

{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|qq=}} .

* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872|qq=}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 17|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Education (Japan)|qq=}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|qq=}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yushima Seidō|qq=}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yushima Seidō Exposition|qq=}} ).

{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|700|ja|700年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/700|qq=}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 3|qq=}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōshō|qq=}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō (era)|qq=}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/710|qq=}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 13|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara-kyō|qq=}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan (Japan)|qq=}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/866|qq=}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 28|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtenmon Incident|qq=}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtenmon Gate|qq=}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ban no Yoshio|qq=}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo clan|qq=}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|qq=}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenju (Japan)|qq=}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1378|qq=}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 7|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|qq=}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|qq=}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.

* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji (era)|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1869|qq=}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 21|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo City|qq=}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|qq=}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
