{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|700|ja|700年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/700|qq=}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 3|qq=}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōshō|qq=}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō (era)|qq=}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/710|qq=}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 13|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara-kyō|qq=}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan (Japan)|qq=}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/866|qq=}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 28|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtenmon Incident|qq=}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtenmon Gate|qq=}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ban no Yoshio|qq=}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo clan|qq=}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|qq=}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenju (Japan)|qq=}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1378|qq=}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 7|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|qq=}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|qq=}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|qq=}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1839|qq=}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 23|qq=}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|qq=}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|qq=}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji (era)|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1869|qq=}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 21|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo City|qq=}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|qq=}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872|qq=}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 17|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Education (Japan)|qq=}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|qq=}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yushima Seidō|qq=}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yushima Seidō Exposition|qq=}} ).

{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|700|ja|700年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/700|qq=}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 3|qq=}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōshō|qq=}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō (era)|qq=}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/710|qq=}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 13|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara-kyō|qq=}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan (Japan)|qq=}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/866|qq=}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 28|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtenmon Incident|qq=}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtenmon Gate|qq=}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ban no Yoshio|qq=}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo clan|qq=}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|qq=}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenju (Japan)|qq=}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1378|qq=}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 7|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|qq=}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|qq=}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|qq=}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1839|qq=}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 23|qq=}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|qq=}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|qq=}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji (era)|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1869|qq=}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 21|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo City|qq=}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|qq=}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872|qq=}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 17|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Education (Japan)|qq=}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|qq=}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yushima Seidō|qq=}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yushima Seidō Exposition|qq=}} ).

== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/700|qq=}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 3|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōshō|qq=}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku (era)|qq=}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/790|qq=}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 28|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Otomuro|qq=}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kanmu|qq=}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/760|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū (era)|qq=}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1191|qq=}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 5|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Nagakata|qq=}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1139|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|qq=}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1544|qq=}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 10|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzōji Iekane|qq=}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu|qq=}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1454|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei (era)|qq=}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1637|qq=}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 5|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honda Masazumi|qq=}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|qq=}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1565|qq=}} ).

[[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[de:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[sn:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:三月初十]] [[en:draft:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ja:和銅3rd year のEvents]] [[sn:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[en:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[sn:arch 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ar:March 10 (Lunisolar)]]

{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|700|ja|700年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/700|qq=}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 3|qq=}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōshō|qq=}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō (era)|qq=}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/710|qq=}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 13|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara-kyō|qq=}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan (Japan)|qq=}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/866|qq=}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 28|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtenmon Incident|qq=}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtenmon Gate|qq=}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ban no Yoshio|qq=}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo clan|qq=}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|qq=}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenju (Japan)|qq=}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1378|qq=}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 7|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|qq=}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|qq=}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|qq=}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1839|qq=}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 23|qq=}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|qq=}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|qq=}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji (era)|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1869|qq=}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 21|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo City|qq=}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|qq=}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872|qq=}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 17|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Education (Japan)|qq=}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|qq=}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yushima Seidō|qq=}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yushima Seidō Exposition|qq=}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshō (era)|qq=}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1134|qq=}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 6|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasuda Yoshisada|qq=}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1194|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|qq=}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1536|qq=}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 31|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshiteru|qq=}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogun|qq=}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1565|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei (era)|qq=}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822|qq=}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 11|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikoma Jōun|qq=}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bakufu|qq=}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/journalist|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1897|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaei (era)|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1848|qq=}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 13|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asano Sōichirō|qq=}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asano zaibatsu|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1930|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō (era)|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1866|qq=}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Kikujirō|qq=}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/diplomat|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji (era)|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1871|qq=}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 29|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|qq=}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Women's Medical University|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1959|qq=}} ).

[[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[de:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[sn:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:三月初十]] [[en:draft:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ja:和銅3rd year のEvents]] [[sn:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[en:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[sn:arch 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ar:March 10 (Lunisolar)]]

{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|700|ja|700年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/700|qq=}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 3|qq=}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōshō|qq=}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|ja|和銅|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō (era)|qq=}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|ja|710年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/710|qq=}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 13|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara-kyō|qq=}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan (Japan)|qq=}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|ja|866年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/866|qq=}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 28|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtenmon Incident|qq=}} : The {{ill|Ōtenmon Gate|ja|応天門|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtenmon Gate|qq=}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Ban no Yoshio|ja|伴善男|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ban no Yoshio|qq=}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo clan|qq=}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|qq=}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju (Japan)|ja|天授 (日本)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenju (Japan)|qq=}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|ja|1378年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1378|qq=}} {{ill|April 7|ja|4月7日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 7|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|qq=}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|qq=}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|qq=}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|1839|ja|1839年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1839|qq=}} {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 23|qq=}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|qq=}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|qq=}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji (era)|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1869|qq=}} {{ill|April 21|ja|4月21日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 21|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|ja|東京市|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo City|qq=}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|qq=}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|ja|1872年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872|qq=}} {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 17|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education (Japan)|ja|文部省|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Education (Japan)|qq=}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|qq=}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yushima Seidō|qq=}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|ja|湯島聖堂博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yushima Seidō Exposition|qq=}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō (era)|ja|長承|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshō (era)|qq=}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|ja|1134年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1134|qq=}} {{ill|April 6|ja|4月6日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 6|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|ja|安田義定|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasuda Yoshisada|qq=}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1194|ja|1194年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1194|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|qq=}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|ja|1536年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1536|qq=}} {{ill|March 31|ja|3月31日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 31|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|ja|足利義輝|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshiteru|qq=}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogun|qq=}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1565|ja|1565年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1565|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei (era)|qq=}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|ja|1822年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822|qq=}} {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 11|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Kurikoma Jōun|ja|栗本鋤雲|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikoma Jōun|qq=}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bakufu|qq=}} official and {{ill|journalist|ja|ジャーナリスト|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/journalist|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1897|ja|1897年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1897|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei (era)|ja|嘉永|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaei (era)|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|ja|1848年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1848|qq=}} {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 13|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|ja|浅野総一郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asano Sōichirō|qq=}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|ja|浅野財閥|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asano zaibatsu|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1930|ja|1930年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1930|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō (era)|ja|慶応|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō (era)|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1866|qq=}} {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|ja|石井菊次郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Kikujirō|qq=}} , Japanese {{ill|diplomat|ja|外交官|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/diplomat|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1945|ja|1945年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji (era)|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji (era)|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|ja|1871年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1871|qq=}} {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 29|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|qq=}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|ja|東京女子医科大学|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Women's Medical University|qq=}} (+ {{ill|1959|ja|1959年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1959|qq=}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|ja|700年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/700|qq=}} {{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 3|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|ja|道昭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōshō|qq=}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku (era)|ja|延暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku (era)|qq=}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|ja|790年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/790|qq=}} {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 28|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|ja|藤原乙牟漏|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Otomuro|qq=}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kanmu|qq=}} (* {{ill|760|ja|760年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/760|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū (era)|ja|建久|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū (era)|qq=}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|ja|1191年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1191|qq=}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 5|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Nagakata|qq=}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|ja|1139年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1139|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|qq=}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|ja|1544年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1544|qq=}} {{ill|April 10|ja|4月10日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 10|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzōji Iekane|qq=}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu|qq=}} (* {{ill|1454|ja|1454年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1454|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei (era)|ja|寛永|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei (era)|qq=}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|ja|1637年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1637|qq=}} {{ill|April 5|ja|4月5日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 5|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|ja|本多正純|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honda Masazumi|qq=}} , early Edo-period {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|qq=}} (* {{ill|1565|ja|1565年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1565|qq=}} ).

{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] 4 ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – Buddhist monk [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:710|710]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}} relocates the capital from [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|qq=}} .
* [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:866|866]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Ōtenmon Incident|Ōtenmon Incident]] : The [[:en:Yingtian Gate|Ōtenmon Gate]] is set ablaze. [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Ban no Yoshio]] , a descendant of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|qq=}} 4 / [[:en:Tenju|Tenju (Japan)]] 4 (Julian [[:en:1378|1378]] [[:en:April 7|April 7]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|qq=}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|qq=}} 19 ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1839|1839]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]] ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|qq=}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|qq=}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.

== Events ==
* [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] 4 ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – Buddhist monk [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:710|710]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}} relocates the capital from [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|qq=}} .

* [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku (era)]] 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:790|790]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Fujiwara no Otomuro|Fujiwara no Otomuro]] , Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] (* [[:en:760|760]] ).
* [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū (era)]] 2 (Julian [[:en:1191|1191]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Nagakata|qq=}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* [[:en:1139|1139]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|qq=}} 15 (Julian [[:en:1544|1544]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]] ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzōji Iekane|qq=}} , feudal lord of [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] (* [[:en:1454|1454]] ).
* [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei (era)]] 14 (Gregorian [[:en:1637|1637]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – [[:en:Honda Masazumi|Honda Masazumi]] , early Edo-period [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] (* [[:en:1565|1565]] ).

[[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[de:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[sn:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 3월 10일]] [[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:三月初十]] [[en:draft:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ja:和銅3rd year のEvents]] [[sn:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[en:3月10日 (旧暦)]] [[sn:arch 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[ar:March 10 (Lunisolar)]] [[Category:Days]] [[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]

{{Not to be confused with|March 10}}<!--hatnote--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] 4 ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – Buddhist monk [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:710|710]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}} relocates the capital from [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|qq=}} .
* [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:866|866]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Ōtenmon Incident|Ōtenmon Incident]] : The [[:en:Yingtian Gate|Ōtenmon Gate]] is set ablaze. [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Ban no Yoshio]] , a descendant of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|qq=}} 4 / [[:en:Tenju|Tenju (Japan)]] 4 (Julian [[:en:1378|1378]] [[:en:April 7|April 7]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|qq=}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|qq=}} 19 ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1839|1839]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]] ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|qq=}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|qq=}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:April 21|April 21]] ) – The [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]] abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|qq=}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ) – The [[:en:Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|Ministry of Education (Japan)]] establishes a museum department (now the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|qq=}} at [[:en:Yushima Seidō|Yushima Seidō]] 's Taiseiden ([[:en:Yushima Seidō Exposition|Yushima Seidō Exposition]] ).
== Births ==
* [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:1134|1134]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]] ) – [[:en:Yasuda Yoshisada|Yasuda Yoshisada]] , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} (+ [[:en:1194|1194]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|qq=}} 5 (Julian [[:en:1536|1536]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiteru|Ashikaga Yoshiteru]] , 13th [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} (+ [[:en:1565|1565]] ).
* [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei (era)]] 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1822|1822]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]] ) – [[:en:Kurimoto Jō'un|Kurikoma Jōun]] , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bakufu|qq=}} official and [[:en:Journalist|journalist]] (+ [[:en:1897|1897]] ).
* [[:en:Kaei|Kaei (era)]] 1 (Gregorian [[:en:1848|1848]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – [[:en:Asano Sōichirō|Asano Sōichirō]] , entrepreneur and founder of the [[:en:Asano zaibatsu|Asano zaibatsu]] (+ [[:en:1930|1930]] ).
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō (era)]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1866|1866]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]] ) – [[:en:Ishii Kikujirō|Ishii Kikujirō]] , Japanese [[:en:Diplomat|diplomat]] (+ [[:en:1945|1945]] ).
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 (Gregorian [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|qq=}} , physician and founder of [[:en:Tokyo Women's Medical University|Tokyo Women's Medical University]] (+ [[:en:1959|1959]] ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] , Buddhist monk.
* [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku (era)]] 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:790|790]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Fujiwara no Otomuro|Fujiwara no Otomuro]] , Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] (* [[:en:760|760]] ).

* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|qq=}} 15 (Julian [[:en:1544|1544]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]] ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzōji Iekane|qq=}} , feudal lord of [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] (* [[:en:1454|1454]] ).
* [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei (era)]] 14 (Gregorian [[:en:1637|1637]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – [[:en:Honda Masazumi|Honda Masazumi]] , early Edo-period [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] (* [[:en:1565|1565]] ).

Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] 4 ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – Buddhist monk [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:710|710]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}} relocates the capital from [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|qq=}} .
* [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:866|866]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Ōtenmon Incident|Ōtenmon Incident]] : The [[:en:Yingtian Gate|Ōtenmon Gate]] is set ablaze. [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Ban no Yoshio]] , a descendant of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|qq=}} 4 / [[:en:Tenju|Tenju (Japan)]] 4 (Julian [[:en:1378|1378]] [[:en:April 7|April 7]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|qq=}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|qq=}} 19 ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1839|1839]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]] ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|qq=}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|qq=}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:April 21|April 21]] ) – The [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]] abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|qq=}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ) – The [[:en:Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|Ministry of Education (Japan)]] establishes a museum department (now the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|qq=}} at [[:en:Yushima Seidō|Yushima Seidō]] 's Taiseiden ([[:en:Yushima Seidō Exposition|Yushima Seidō Exposition]] ).
== Births ==
* [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:1134|1134]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]] ) – [[:en:Yasuda Yoshisada|Yasuda Yoshisada]] , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} (+ [[:en:1194|1194]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|qq=}} 5 (Julian [[:en:1536|1536]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiteru|Ashikaga Yoshiteru]] , 13th [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} (+ [[:en:1565|1565]] ).
* [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei (era)]] 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1822|1822]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]] ) – [[:en:Kurimoto Jō'un|Kurikoma Jōun]] , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bakufu|qq=}} official and [[:en:Journalist|journalist]] (+ [[:en:1897|1897]] ).
* [[:en:Kaei|Kaei (era)]] 1 (Gregorian [[:en:1848|1848]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – [[:en:Asano Sōichirō|Asano Sōichirō]] , entrepreneur and founder of the [[:en:Asano zaibatsu|Asano zaibatsu]] (+ [[:en:1930|1930]] ).
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō (era)]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1866|1866]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]] ) – [[:en:Ishii Kikujirō|Ishii Kikujirō]] , Japanese [[:en:Diplomat|diplomat]] (+ [[:en:1945|1945]] ).
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 (Gregorian [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|qq=}} , physician and founder of [[:en:Tokyo Women's Medical University|Tokyo Women's Medical University]] (+ [[:en:1959|1959]] ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] , Buddhist monk.
* [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku (era)]] 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:790|790]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Fujiwara no Otomuro|Fujiwara no Otomuro]] , Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] (* [[:en:760|760]] ).
* [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū (era)]] 2 (Julian [[:en:1191|1191]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Nagakata|qq=}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* [[:en:1139|1139]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|qq=}} 15 (Julian [[:en:1544|1544]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]] ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzōji Iekane|qq=}} , feudal lord of [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] (* [[:en:1454|1454]] ).
* [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei (era)]] 14 (Gregorian [[:en:1637|1637]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – [[:en:Honda Masazumi|Honda Masazumi]] , early Edo-period [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] (* [[:en:1565|1565]] ).
== Commemorations ==
== See Also ==

Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|qq=}} .

* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|qq=}} 4 / [[:en:Tenju|Tenju (Japan)]] 4 (Julian [[:en:1378|1378]] [[:en:April 7|April 7]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|qq=}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.

* Meiji 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ) – The [[:en:Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|Ministry of Education (Japan)]] establishes a museum department (now the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|qq=}} at [[:en:Yushima Seidō|Yushima Seidō]] 's Taiseiden ([[:en:Yushima Seidō Exposition|Yushima Seidō Exposition]] ).

* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|qq=}} 5 (Julian [[:en:1536|1536]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiteru|Ashikaga Yoshiteru]] , 13th [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} (+ [[:en:1565|1565]] ).

* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 (Gregorian [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|qq=}} , physician and founder of [[:en:Tokyo Women's Medical University|Tokyo Women's Medical University]] (+ [[:en:1959|1959]] ).

* Meiji 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ) – The [[:en:Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|Ministry of Education (Japan)]] establishes a museum department (now the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|qq=}} at [[:en:Yushima Seidō|Yushima Seidō]] 's Taiseiden ([[:en:Yushima Seidō Exposition|Yushima Seidō Exposition]] ).
== Births ==
* [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:1134|1134]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]] ) – [[:en:Yasuda Yoshisada|Yasuda Yoshisada]] , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} (+ [[:en:1194|1194]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|qq=}} 5 (Julian [[:en:1536|1536]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiteru|Ashikaga Yoshiteru]] , 13th [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} (+ [[:en:1565|1565]] ).
* [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei (era)]] 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1822|1822]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]] ) – [[:en:Kurimoto Jō'un|Kurikoma Jōun]] , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bakufu|qq=}} official and [[:en:Journalist|journalist]] (+ [[:en:1897|1897]] ).
* [[:en:Kaei|Kaei (era)]] 1 (Gregorian [[:en:1848|1848]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – [[:en:Asano Sōichirō|Asano Sōichirō]] , entrepreneur and founder of the [[:en:Asano zaibatsu|Asano zaibatsu]] (+ [[:en:1930|1930]] ).
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō (era)]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1866|1866]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]] ) – [[:en:Ishii Kikujirō|Ishii Kikujirō]] , Japanese [[:en:Diplomat|diplomat]] (+ [[:en:1945|1945]] ).
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 (Gregorian [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|qq=}} , physician and founder of [[:en:Tokyo Women's Medical University|Tokyo Women's Medical University]] (+ [[:en:1959|1959]] ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] , Buddhist monk.
* [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku (era)]] 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:790|790]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Fujiwara no Otomuro|Fujiwara no Otomuro]] , Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] (* [[:en:760|760]] ).
* [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū (era)]] 2 (Julian [[:en:1191|1191]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Nagakata|qq=}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* [[:en:1139|1139]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|qq=}} 15 (Julian [[:en:1544|1544]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]] ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzōji Iekane|qq=}} , feudal lord of [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] (* [[:en:1454|1454]] ).
* [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei (era)]] 14 (Gregorian [[:en:1637|1637]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – [[:en:Honda Masazumi|Honda Masazumi]] , early Edo-period [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] (* [[:en:1565|1565]] ).

{{Not to be confused with|March 10|qq=March 10}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] 4 ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – Buddhist monk [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:710|710]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}} relocates the capital from [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|qq=}} .
* [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:866|866]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Ōtenmon Incident|Ōtenmon Incident]] : The [[:en:Yingtian Gate|Ōtenmon Gate]] is set ablaze. [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Ban no Yoshio]] , a descendant of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|qq=}} 4 / [[:en:Tenju|Tenju (Japan)]] 4 (Julian [[:en:1378|1378]] [[:en:April 7|April 7]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|qq=}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|qq=}} 19 ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1839|1839]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]] ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|qq=}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|qq=}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:April 21|April 21]] ) – The [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]] abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|qq=}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ) – The [[:en:Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|Ministry of Education (Japan)]] establishes a museum department (now the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|qq=}} at [[:en:Yushima Seidō|Yushima Seidō]] 's Taiseiden ([[:en:Yushima Seidō Exposition|Yushima Seidō Exposition]] ).
== Births ==
* [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:1134|1134]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]] ) – [[:en:Yasuda Yoshisada|Yasuda Yoshisada]] , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} (+ [[:en:1194|1194]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|qq=}} 5 (Julian [[:en:1536|1536]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiteru|Ashikaga Yoshiteru]] , 13th [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} (+ [[:en:1565|1565]] ).
* [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei (era)]] 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1822|1822]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]] ) – [[:en:Kurimoto Jō'un|Kurikoma Jōun]] , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bakufu|qq=}} official and [[:en:Journalist|journalist]] (+ [[:en:1897|1897]] ).
* [[:en:Kaei|Kaei (era)]] 1 (Gregorian [[:en:1848|1848]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – [[:en:Asano Sōichirō|Asano Sōichirō]] , entrepreneur and founder of the [[:en:Asano zaibatsu|Asano zaibatsu]] (+ [[:en:1930|1930]] ).
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō (era)]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1866|1866]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]] ) – [[:en:Ishii Kikujirō|Ishii Kikujirō]] , Japanese [[:en:Diplomat|diplomat]] (+ [[:en:1945|1945]] ).
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 (Gregorian [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|qq=}} , physician and founder of [[:en:Tokyo Women's Medical University|Tokyo Women's Medical University]] (+ [[:en:1959|1959]] ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] , Buddhist monk.
* [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku (era)]] 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:790|790]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Fujiwara no Otomuro|Fujiwara no Otomuro]] , Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] (* [[:en:760|760]] ).
* [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū (era)]] 2 (Julian [[:en:1191|1191]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Nagakata|qq=}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* [[:en:1139|1139]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|qq=}} 15 (Julian [[:en:1544|1544]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]] ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzōji Iekane|qq=}} , feudal lord of [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] (* [[:en:1454|1454]] ).
* [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei (era)]] 14 (Gregorian [[:en:1637|1637]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – [[:en:Honda Masazumi|Honda Masazumi]] , early Edo-period [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] (* [[:en:1565|1565]] ).

{{Not to be confused with|March 10|qq=March 10}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] 4 ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – Buddhist monk [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:710|710]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}} relocates the capital from [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|qq=}} .
* [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:866|866]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Ōtenmon Incident|Ōtenmon Incident]] : The [[:en:Yingtian Gate|Ōtenmon Gate]] is set ablaze. [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Ban no Yoshio]] , a descendant of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|qq=}} 4 / [[:en:Tenju|Tenju (Japan)]] 4 (Julian [[:en:1378|1378]] [[:en:April 7|April 7]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|qq=}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|qq=}} 19 ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1839|1839]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]] ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|qq=}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|qq=}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:April 21|April 21]] ) – The [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]] abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|qq=}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ) – The [[:en:Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|Ministry of Education (Japan)]] establishes a museum department (now the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|qq=}} at [[:en:Yushima Seidō|Yushima Seidō]] 's Taiseiden ([[:en:Yushima Seidō Exposition|Yushima Seidō Exposition]] ).
== Births ==
* [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:1134|1134]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]] ) – [[:en:Yasuda Yoshisada|Yasuda Yoshisada]] , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} (+ [[:en:1194|1194]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|qq=}} 5 (Julian [[:en:1536|1536]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiteru|Ashikaga Yoshiteru]] , 13th [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|qq=}} (+ [[:en:1565|1565]] ).
* [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei (era)]] 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1822|1822]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]] ) – [[:en:Kurimoto Jō'un|Kurikoma Jōun]] , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bakufu|qq=}} official and [[:en:Journalist|journalist]] (+ [[:en:1897|1897]] ).
* [[:en:Kaei|Kaei (era)]] 1 (Gregorian [[:en:1848|1848]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – [[:en:Asano Sōichirō|Asano Sōichirō]] , entrepreneur and founder of the [[:en:Asano zaibatsu|Asano zaibatsu]] (+ [[:en:1930|1930]] ).
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō (era)]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1866|1866]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]] ) – [[:en:Ishii Kikujirō|Ishii Kikujirō]] , Japanese [[:en:Diplomat|diplomat]] (+ [[:en:1945|1945]] ).
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 (Gregorian [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|qq=}} , physician and founder of [[:en:Tokyo Women's Medical University|Tokyo Women's Medical University]] (+ [[:en:1959|1959]] ).
== Deaths ==

* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|qq=}} 15 (Julian [[:en:1544|1544]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]] ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzōji Iekane|qq=}} , feudal lord of [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] (* [[:en:1454|1454]] ).
* [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei (era)]] 14 (Gregorian [[:en:1637|1637]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – [[:en:Honda Masazumi|Honda Masazumi]] , early Edo-period [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] (* [[:en:1565|1565]] ).
