[[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Ban no Yoshio]] , a descendant of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 4 / [[:en:Tenju|Tenju (Japan)]] 4 (Julian [[:en:1378|1378]] [[:en:April 7|April 7]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|1=Daoguang Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1133186}} 19 ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1839|1839]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]] ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広東省|1=Guangdong|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15175}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヘン|1=opium|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q46452}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:April 21|April 21]] ) – The [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]] abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 庄屋|1=shōya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095960}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.

== Births ==
* [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:1134|1134]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]] ) – [[:en:Yasuda Yoshisada|Yasuda Yoshisada]] , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=military commander|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545923}} (+ [[:en:1194|1194]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 5 (Julian [[:en:1536|1536]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiteru|Ashikaga Yoshiteru]] , 13th [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} (+ [[:en:1565|1565]] ).
* [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei (era)]] 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1822|1822]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]] ) – [[:en:Kurimoto Jō'un|Kurikoma Jōun]] , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bakufu|1=bakushin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481849}} official and [[:en:Journalist|journalist]] (+ [[:en:1897|1897]] ).
* [[:en:Kaei|Kaei (era)]] 1 (Gregorian [[:en:1848|1848]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – [[:en:Asano Sōichirō|Asano Sōichirō]] , entrepreneur and founder of the [[:en:Asano zaibatsu|Asano zaibatsu]] (+ [[:en:1930|1930]] ).
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō (era)]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1866|1866]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]] ) – [[:en:Ishii Kikujirō|Ishii Kikujirō]] , Japanese [[:en:Diplomat|diplomat]] (+ [[:en:1945|1945]] ).
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 (Gregorian [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉岡彌生|1=Yoshioka Yayoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138424}} , physician and founder of [[:en:Tokyo Women's Medical University|Tokyo Women's Medical University]] (+ [[:en:1959|1959]] ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] , Buddhist monk.
* [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku (era)]] 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:790|790]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Fujiwara no Otomuro|Fujiwara no Otomuro]] , Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] (* [[:en:760|760]] ).
* [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū (era)]] 2 (Julian [[:en:1191|1191]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Nagakata|1=Nagakata Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623620}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* [[:en:1139|1139]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 15 (Julian [[:en:1544|1544]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]] ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzōji Iekane|1=Ryūzōji Iekane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5368164}} , feudal lord of [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] (* [[:en:1454|1454]] ).
* [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei (era)]] 14 (Gregorian [[:en:1637|1637]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – [[:en:Honda Masazumi|Honda Masazumi]] , early Edo-period [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] (* [[:en:1565|1565]] ).

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 10일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初十]][[zh:三月初十]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839021]] Wikidata name: 10th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 10|qq=March 10}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|1=Shakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635368}} .
== Events ==
* [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] 4 ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – Buddhist monk [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:710|710]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocates the capital from [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} .
* [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:866|866]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Ōtenmon Incident|Ōtenmon Incident]] : The [[:en:Yingtian Gate|Ōtenmon Gate]] is set ablaze. [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Ban no Yoshio]] , a descendant of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 4 / [[:en:Tenju|Tenju (Japan)]] 4 (Julian [[:en:1378|1378]] [[:en:April 7|April 7]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|1=Daoguang Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1133186}} 19 ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1839|1839]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]] ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広東省|1=Guangdong|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15175}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヘン|1=opium|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q46452}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:April 21|April 21]] ) – The [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]] abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 庄屋|1=shōya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095960}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ) – The [[:en:Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|Ministry of Education (Japan)]] establishes a museum department (now the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|1=hakurankai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112298281}} at [[:en:Yushima Seidō|Yushima Seidō]] 's Taiseiden ([[:en:Yushima Seidō Exposition|Yushima Seidō Exposition]] ).
== Births ==
* [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:1134|1134]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]] ) – [[:en:Yasuda Yoshisada|Yasuda Yoshisada]] , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=military commander|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545923}} (+ [[:en:1194|1194]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 5 (Julian [[:en:1536|1536]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiteru|Ashikaga Yoshiteru]] , 13th [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} (+ [[:en:1565|1565]] ).
* [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei (era)]] 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1822|1822]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]] ) – [[:en:Kurimoto Jō'un|Kurikoma Jōun]] , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bakufu|1=bakushin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481849}} official and [[:en:Journalist|journalist]] (+ [[:en:1897|1897]] ).
* [[:en:Kaei|Kaei (era)]] 1 (Gregorian [[:en:1848|1848]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – [[:en:Asano Sōichirō|Asano Sōichirō]] , entrepreneur and founder of the [[:en:Asano zaibatsu|Asano zaibatsu]] (+ [[:en:1930|1930]] ).
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō (era)]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1866|1866]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]] ) – [[:en:Ishii Kikujirō|Ishii Kikujirō]] , Japanese [[:en:Diplomat|diplomat]] (+ [[:en:1945|1945]] ).
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 (Gregorian [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉岡彌生|1=Yoshioka Yayoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138424}} , physician and founder of [[:en:Tokyo Women's Medical University|Tokyo Women's Medical University]] (+ [[:en:1959|1959]] ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] , Buddhist monk.
* [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku (era)]] 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:790|790]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Fujiwara no Otomuro|Fujiwara no Otomuro]] , Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] (* [[:en:760|760]] ).
* [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū (era)]] 2 (Julian [[:en:1191|1191]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Nagakata|1=Nagakata Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623620}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* [[:en:1139|1139]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 15 (Julian [[:en:1544|1544]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]] ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzōji Iekane|1=Ryūzōji Iekane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5368164}} , feudal lord of [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] (* [[:en:1454|1454]] ).
* [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei (era)]] 14 (Gregorian [[:en:1637|1637]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – [[:en:Honda Masazumi|Honda Masazumi]] , early Edo-period [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] (* [[:en:1565|1565]] ).

* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 (Gregorian [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉岡彌生|1=Yoshioka Yayoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138424}} , physician and founder of [[:en:Tokyo Women's Medical University|Tokyo Women's Medical University]] (+ [[:en:1959|1959]] ).
== Deaths ==

It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|1=Shakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635368}} .
== Events ==
* [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] 4 ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – Buddhist monk [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:710|710]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocates the capital from [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} .
* [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:866|866]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Ōtenmon Incident|Ōtenmon Incident]] : The [[:en:Yingtian Gate|Ōtenmon Gate]] is set ablaze. [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Ban no Yoshio]] , a descendant of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 4 / [[:en:Tenju|Tenju (Japan)]] 4 (Julian [[:en:1378|1378]] [[:en:April 7|April 7]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|1=Daoguang Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1133186}} 19 ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1839|1839]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]] ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広東省|1=Guangdong|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15175}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヘン|1=opium|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q46452}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:April 21|April 21]] ) – The [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]] abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 庄屋|1=shōya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095960}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ) – The [[:en:Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|Ministry of Education (Japan)]] establishes a museum department (now the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|1=hakurankai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112298281}} at [[:en:Yushima Seidō|Yushima Seidō]] 's Taiseiden ([[:en:Yushima Seidō Exposition|Yushima Seidō Exposition]] ).

* [[:en:Kaei|Kaei (era)]] 1 (Gregorian [[:en:1848|1848]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – [[:en:Asano Sōichirō|Asano Sōichirō]] , entrepreneur and founder of the [[:en:Asano zaibatsu|Asano zaibatsu]] (+ [[:en:1930|1930]] ).
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō (era)]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1866|1866]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]] ) – [[:en:Ishii Kikujirō|Ishii Kikujirō]] , Japanese [[:en:Diplomat|diplomat]] (+ [[:en:1945|1945]] ).
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 (Gregorian [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉岡彌生|1=Yoshioka Yayoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138424}} , physician and founder of [[:en:Tokyo Women's Medical University|Tokyo Women's Medical University]] (+ [[:en:1959|1959]] ).

* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 (Gregorian [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉岡彌生|1=Yoshioka Yayoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138424}} , physician and founder of [[:en:Tokyo Women's Medical University|Tokyo Women's Medical University]] (+ [[:en:1959|1959]] ).

It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|1=Shakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635368}} .
== Events ==
* [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] 4 ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – Buddhist monk [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:710|710]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocates the capital from [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} .
* [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:866|866]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Ōtenmon Incident|Ōtenmon Incident]] : The [[:en:Yingtian Gate|Ōtenmon Gate]] is set ablaze. [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Ban no Yoshio]] , a descendant of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 4 / [[:en:Tenju|Tenju (Japan)]] 4 (Julian [[:en:1378|1378]] [[:en:April 7|April 7]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|1=Daoguang Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1133186}} 19 ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1839|1839]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]] ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広東省|1=Guangdong|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15175}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヘン|1=opium|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q46452}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:April 21|April 21]] ) – The [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]] abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 庄屋|1=shōya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095960}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ) – The [[:en:Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|Ministry of Education (Japan)]] establishes a museum department (now the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|1=hakurankai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112298281}} at [[:en:Yushima Seidō|Yushima Seidō]] 's Taiseiden ([[:en:Yushima Seidō Exposition|Yushima Seidō Exposition]] ).

* [[:en:Kaei|Kaei (era)]] 1 (Gregorian [[:en:1848|1848]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – [[:en:Asano Sōichirō|Asano Sōichirō]] , entrepreneur and founder of the [[:en:Asano zaibatsu|Asano zaibatsu]] (+ [[:en:1930|1930]] ).
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō (era)]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1866|1866]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]] ) – [[:en:Ishii Kikujirō|Ishii Kikujirō]] , Japanese [[:en:Diplomat|diplomat]] (+ [[:en:1945|1945]] ).
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 (Gregorian [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉岡彌生|1=Yoshioka Yayoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138424}} , physician and founder of [[:en:Tokyo Women's Medical University|Tokyo Women's Medical University]] (+ [[:en:1959|1959]] ).

* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 15 (Julian [[:en:1544|1544]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]] ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzōji Iekane|1=Ryūzōji Iekane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5368164}} , feudal lord of [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] (* [[:en:1454|1454]] ).

* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 (Gregorian [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉岡彌生|1=Yoshioka Yayoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138424}} , physician and founder of [[:en:Tokyo Women's Medical University|Tokyo Women's Medical University]] (+ [[:en:1959|1959]] ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] , Buddhist monk.
* [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku (era)]] 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:790|790]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Fujiwara no Otomuro|Fujiwara no Otomuro]] , Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] (* [[:en:760|760]] ).
* [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū (era)]] 2 (Julian [[:en:1191|1191]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Nagakata|1=Nagakata Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623620}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* [[:en:1139|1139]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 15 (Julian [[:en:1544|1544]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]] ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzōji Iekane|1=Ryūzōji Iekane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5368164}} , feudal lord of [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] (* [[:en:1454|1454]] ).
* [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei (era)]] 14 (Gregorian [[:en:1637|1637]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – [[:en:Honda Masazumi|Honda Masazumi]] , early Edo-period [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] (* [[:en:1565|1565]] ).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 10일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初十]][[zh:三月初十]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839021]] Wikidata name: 10th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 10|qq=March 10}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|1=Shakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635368}} .
== Events ==
* [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] 4 ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – Buddhist monk [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:710|710]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocates the capital from [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} .
* [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:866|866]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Ōtenmon Incident|Ōtenmon Incident]] : The [[:en:Yingtian Gate|Ōtenmon Gate]] is set ablaze. [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Ban no Yoshio]] , a descendant of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 4 / [[:en:Tenju|Tenju (Japan)]] 4 (Julian [[:en:1378|1378]] [[:en:April 7|April 7]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|1=Daoguang Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1133186}} 19 ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1839|1839]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]] ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広東省|1=Guangdong|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15175}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヘン|1=opium|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q46452}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:April 21|April 21]] ) – The [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]] abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 庄屋|1=shōya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095960}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ) – The [[:en:Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|Ministry of Education (Japan)]] establishes a museum department (now the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|1=hakurankai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112298281}} at [[:en:Yushima Seidō|Yushima Seidō]] 's Taiseiden ([[:en:Yushima Seidō Exposition|Yushima Seidō Exposition]] ).
== Births ==
* [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:1134|1134]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]] ) – [[:en:Yasuda Yoshisada|Yasuda Yoshisada]] , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=military commander|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545923}} (+ [[:en:1194|1194]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 5 (Julian [[:en:1536|1536]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiteru|Ashikaga Yoshiteru]] , 13th [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} (+ [[:en:1565|1565]] ).
* [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei (era)]] 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1822|1822]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]] ) – [[:en:Kurimoto Jō'un|Kurikoma Jōun]] , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bakufu|1=bakushin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481849}} official and [[:en:Journalist|journalist]] (+ [[:en:1897|1897]] ).
* [[:en:Kaei|Kaei (era)]] 1 (Gregorian [[:en:1848|1848]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – [[:en:Asano Sōichirō|Asano Sōichirō]] , entrepreneur and founder of the [[:en:Asano zaibatsu|Asano zaibatsu]] (+ [[:en:1930|1930]] ).
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō (era)]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1866|1866]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]] ) – [[:en:Ishii Kikujirō|Ishii Kikujirō]] , Japanese [[:en:Diplomat|diplomat]] (+ [[:en:1945|1945]] ).
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 (Gregorian [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉岡彌生|1=Yoshioka Yayoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138424}} , physician and founder of [[:en:Tokyo Women's Medical University|Tokyo Women's Medical University]] (+ [[:en:1959|1959]] ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] , Buddhist monk.
* [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku (era)]] 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:790|790]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Fujiwara no Otomuro|Fujiwara no Otomuro]] , Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] (* [[:en:760|760]] ).
* [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū (era)]] 2 (Julian [[:en:1191|1191]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Nagakata|1=Nagakata Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623620}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* [[:en:1139|1139]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 15 (Julian [[:en:1544|1544]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]] ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzōji Iekane|1=Ryūzōji Iekane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5368164}} , feudal lord of [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] (* [[:en:1454|1454]] ).
* [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei (era)]] 14 (Gregorian [[:en:1637|1637]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – [[:en:Honda Masazumi|Honda Masazumi]] , early Edo-period [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] (* [[:en:1565|1565]] ).

* [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] 4 ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – Buddhist monk [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.

[[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Ban no Yoshio]] , a descendant of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 4 / [[:en:Tenju|Tenju (Japan)]] 4 (Julian [[:en:1378|1378]] [[:en:April 7|April 7]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.

== Births ==
* [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:1134|1134]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]] ) – [[:en:Yasuda Yoshisada|Yasuda Yoshisada]] , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=military commander|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545923}} (+ [[:en:1194|1194]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 5 (Julian [[:en:1536|1536]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiteru|Ashikaga Yoshiteru]] , 13th [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} (+ [[:en:1565|1565]] ).
* [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei (era)]] 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1822|1822]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]] ) – [[:en:Kurimoto Jō'un|Kurikoma Jōun]] , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bakufu|1=bakushin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481849}} official and [[:en:Journalist|journalist]] (+ [[:en:1897|1897]] ).
* [[:en:Kaei|Kaei (era)]] 1 (Gregorian [[:en:1848|1848]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – [[:en:Asano Sōichirō|Asano Sōichirō]] , entrepreneur and founder of the [[:en:Asano zaibatsu|Asano zaibatsu]] (+ [[:en:1930|1930]] ).
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō (era)]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1866|1866]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]] ) – [[:en:Ishii Kikujirō|Ishii Kikujirō]] , Japanese [[:en:Diplomat|diplomat]] (+ [[:en:1945|1945]] ).
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 (Gregorian [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉岡彌生|1=Yoshioka Yayoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138424}} , physician and founder of [[:en:Tokyo Women's Medical University|Tokyo Women's Medical University]] (+ [[:en:1959|1959]] ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] , Buddhist monk.
* [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku (era)]] 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:790|790]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Fujiwara no Otomuro|Fujiwara no Otomuro]] , Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] (* [[:en:760|760]] ).
* [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū (era)]] 2 (Julian [[:en:1191|1191]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Nagakata|1=Nagakata Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623620}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* [[:en:1139|1139]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 15 (Julian [[:en:1544|1544]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]] ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzōji Iekane|1=Ryūzōji Iekane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5368164}} , feudal lord of [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] (* [[:en:1454|1454]] ).
* [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei (era)]] 14 (Gregorian [[:en:1637|1637]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – [[:en:Honda Masazumi|Honda Masazumi]] , early Edo-period [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] (* [[:en:1565|1565]] ).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 10일]][[zh_min_nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:三月初十]][[zh:三月初十]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 10일]][[zh_min_nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:三月初十]][[zh:三月初十]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 10일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初十]][[zh:三月初十]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839021]] Wikidata name: 10th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 10|qq=March 10}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|1=Shakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635368}} .
== Events ==
* [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] 4 ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – Buddhist monk [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:710|710]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocates the capital from [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} .
* [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:866|866]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Ōtenmon Incident|Ōtenmon Incident]] : The [[:en:Yingtian Gate|Ōtenmon Gate]] is set ablaze. [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Ban no Yoshio]] , a descendant of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 4 / [[:en:Tenju|Tenju (Japan)]] 4 (Julian [[:en:1378|1378]] [[:en:April 7|April 7]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|1=Daoguang Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1133186}} 19 ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1839|1839]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]] ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広東省|1=Guangdong|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15175}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヘン|1=opium|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q46452}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:April 21|April 21]] ) – The [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]] abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 庄屋|1=shōya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095960}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ) – The [[:en:Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|Ministry of Education (Japan)]] establishes a museum department (now the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|1=hakurankai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112298281}} at [[:en:Yushima Seidō|Yushima Seidō]] 's Taiseiden ([[:en:Yushima Seidō Exposition|Yushima Seidō Exposition]] ).
== Births ==
* [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:1134|1134]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]] ) – [[:en:Yasuda Yoshisada|Yasuda Yoshisada]] , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=military commander|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545923}} (+ [[:en:1194|1194]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 5 (Julian [[:en:1536|1536]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiteru|Ashikaga Yoshiteru]] , 13th [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} (+ [[:en:1565|1565]] ).
* [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei (era)]] 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1822|1822]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]] ) – [[:en:Kurimoto Jō'un|Kurikoma Jōun]] , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bakufu|1=bakushin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481849}} official and [[:en:Journalist|journalist]] (+ [[:en:1897|1897]] ).
* [[:en:Kaei|Kaei (era)]] 1 (Gregorian [[:en:1848|1848]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – [[:en:Asano Sōichirō|Asano Sōichirō]] , entrepreneur and founder of the [[:en:Asano zaibatsu|Asano zaibatsu]] (+ [[:en:1930|1930]] ).
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō (era)]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1866|1866]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]] ) – [[:en:Ishii Kikujirō|Ishii Kikujirō]] , Japanese [[:en:Diplomat|diplomat]] (+ [[:en:1945|1945]] ).
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 (Gregorian [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉岡彌生|1=Yoshioka Yayoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138424}} , physician and founder of [[:en:Tokyo Women's Medical University|Tokyo Women's Medical University]] (+ [[:en:1959|1959]] ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] , Buddhist monk.
* [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku (era)]] 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:790|790]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Fujiwara no Otomuro|Fujiwara no Otomuro]] , Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] (* [[:en:760|760]] ).
* [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū (era)]] 2 (Julian [[:en:1191|1191]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Nagakata|1=Nagakata Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623620}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* [[:en:1139|1139]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 15 (Julian [[:en:1544|1544]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]] ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzōji Iekane|1=Ryūzōji Iekane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5368164}} , feudal lord of [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] (* [[:en:1454|1454]] ).
* [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei (era)]] 14 (Gregorian [[:en:1637|1637]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – [[:en:Honda Masazumi|Honda Masazumi]] , early Edo-period [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] (* [[:en:1565|1565]] ).

* [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:1134|1134]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]] ) – [[:en:Yasuda Yoshisada|Yasuda Yoshisada]] , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=military commander|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545923}} (+ [[:en:1194|1194]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 5 (Julian [[:en:1536|1536]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiteru|Ashikaga Yoshiteru]] , 13th [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} (+ [[:en:1565|1565]] ).
* [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei (era)]] 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1822|1822]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]] ) – [[:en:Kurimoto Jō'un|Kurikoma Jōun]] , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bakufu|1=bakushin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481849}} official and [[:en:Journalist|journalist]] (+ [[:en:1897|1897]] ).
* [[:en:Kaei|Kaei (era)]] 1 (Gregorian [[:en:1848|1848]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – [[:en:Asano Sōichirō|Asano Sōichirō]] , entrepreneur and founder of the [[:en:Asano zaibatsu|Asano zaibatsu]] (+ [[:en:1930|1930]] ).
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō (era)]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1866|1866]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]] ) – [[:en:Ishii Kikujirō|Ishii Kikujirō]] , Japanese [[:en:Diplomat|diplomat]] (+ [[:en:1945|1945]] ).
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 (Gregorian [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉岡彌生|1=Yoshioka Yayoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138424}} , physician and founder of [[:en:Tokyo Women's Medical University|Tokyo Women's Medical University]] (+ [[:en:1959|1959]] ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] , Buddhist monk.
* [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku (era)]] 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:790|790]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Fujiwara no Otomuro|Fujiwara no Otomuro]] , Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] (* [[:en:760|760]] ).
* [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū (era)]] 2 (Julian [[:en:1191|1191]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Nagakata|1=Nagakata Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623620}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* [[:en:1139|1139]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 15 (Julian [[:en:1544|1544]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]] ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzōji Iekane|1=Ryūzōji Iekane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5368164}} , feudal lord of [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] (* [[:en:1454|1454]] ).
* [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei (era)]] 14 (Gregorian [[:en:1637|1637]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – [[:en:Honda Masazumi|Honda Masazumi]] , early Edo-period [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] (* [[:en:1565|1565]] ).

== Events ==
* [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] 4 ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – Buddhist monk [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:710|710]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocates the capital from [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} .
* [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:866|866]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Ōtenmon Incident|Ōtenmon Incident]] : The [[:en:Yingtian Gate|Ōtenmon Gate]] is set ablaze. [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Ban no Yoshio]] , a descendant of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 4 / [[:en:Tenju|Tenju (Japan)]] 4 (Julian [[:en:1378|1378]] [[:en:April 7|April 7]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|1=Daoguang Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1133186}} 19 ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1839|1839]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]] ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広東省|1=Guangdong|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15175}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヘン|1=opium|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q46452}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:April 21|April 21]] ) – The [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]] abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 庄屋|1=shōya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095960}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ) – The [[:en:Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|Ministry of Education (Japan)]] establishes a museum department (now the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|1=hakurankai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112298281}} at [[:en:Yushima Seidō|Yushima Seidō]] 's Taiseiden ([[:en:Yushima Seidō Exposition|Yushima Seidō Exposition]] ).

== Events ==
* [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] 4 ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – Buddhist monk [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:710|710]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocates the capital from [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} .

[[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Ban no Yoshio]] , a descendant of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 4 / [[:en:Tenju|Tenju (Japan)]] 4 (Julian [[:en:1378|1378]] [[:en:April 7|April 7]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|1=Daoguang Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1133186}} 19 ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1839|1839]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]] ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広東省|1=Guangdong|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15175}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヘン|1=opium|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q46452}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:April 21|April 21]] ) – The [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]] abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 庄屋|1=shōya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095960}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ) – The [[:en:Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|Ministry of Education (Japan)]] establishes a museum department (now the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|1=hakurankai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112298281}} at [[:en:Yushima Seidō|Yushima Seidō]] 's Taiseiden ([[:en:Yushima Seidō Exposition|Yushima Seidō Exposition]] ).
== Births ==
* [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō (era)]] 3 (Julian [[:en:1134|1134]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]] ) – [[:en:Yasuda Yoshisada|Yasuda Yoshisada]] , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=military commander|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545923}} (+ [[:en:1194|1194]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 5 (Julian [[:en:1536|1536]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiteru|Ashikaga Yoshiteru]] , 13th [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} (+ [[:en:1565|1565]] ).
* [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei (era)]] 5 (Gregorian [[:en:1822|1822]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]] ) – [[:en:Kurimoto Jō'un|Kurikoma Jōun]] , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bakufu|1=bakushin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481849}} official and [[:en:Journalist|journalist]] (+ [[:en:1897|1897]] ).
* [[:en:Kaei|Kaei (era)]] 1 (Gregorian [[:en:1848|1848]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]] ) – [[:en:Asano Sōichirō|Asano Sōichirō]] , entrepreneur and founder of the [[:en:Asano zaibatsu|Asano zaibatsu]] (+ [[:en:1930|1930]] ).
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō (era)]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1866|1866]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]] ) – [[:en:Ishii Kikujirō|Ishii Kikujirō]] , Japanese [[:en:Diplomat|diplomat]] (+ [[:en:1945|1945]] ).
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 (Gregorian [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉岡彌生|1=Yoshioka Yayoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138424}} , physician and founder of [[:en:Tokyo Women's Medical University|Tokyo Women's Medical University]] (+ [[:en:1959|1959]] ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian [[:en:700|700]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]] ) – [[:en:Dōshō|Dōshō]] , Buddhist monk.
* [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku (era)]] 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian [[:en:790|790]] [[:en:April 28|April 28]] ) – [[:en:Fujiwara no Otomuro|Fujiwara no Otomuro]] , Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] (* [[:en:760|760]] ).
* [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū (era)]] 2 (Julian [[:en:1191|1191]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Nagakata|1=Nagakata Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623620}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* [[:en:1139|1139]] ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 15 (Julian [[:en:1544|1544]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]] ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzōji Iekane|1=Ryūzōji Iekane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5368164}} , feudal lord of [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] (* [[:en:1454|1454]] ).
* [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei (era)]] 14 (Gregorian [[:en:1637|1637]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]] ) – [[:en:Honda Masazumi|Honda Masazumi]] , early Edo-period [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] (* [[:en:1565|1565]] ).
== Commemorations ==
== See Also ==

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 10일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初十]][[zh:三月初十]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 10일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初十]][[zh:三月初十]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 10 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月10日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 10일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 10 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初十]][[zh:三月初十]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839021]] Wikidata name: 10th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 10|qq=March 10}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 10일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 10''' is the 10th day of month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|1=Shakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635368}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu}} 4 ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|700|en|700|WD=Q23494|lt=700}} {{ill|April 3|en|April 3|WD=Q2509|lt=April 3}} ) – Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōshō|en|Dōshō|WD=Q724005|lt=Dōshō}} is cremated, marking the first recorded cremation in Japan.
* {{ill|Wadō (era)|en|Wadō (era)|WD=Q1373026|lt=Wadō (era)}} 3 (Julian {{ill|710|en|710|WD=Q23518|lt=710}} {{ill|April 13|en|April 13|WD=Q2498|lt=April 13}} ) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|Emperor Genmei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocates the capital from {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|en|Fujiwara-kyō|WD=Q1196381|lt=Fujiwara-kyō}} to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|Heijō-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} .
* {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan (Japan)}} 8 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|866|en|866|WD=Q23872|lt=866}} {{ill|April 28|en|April 28|WD=Q2534|lt=April 28}} ) – {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|en|Ōtenmon Incident|WD=Q169232|lt=Ōtenmon Incident}} : The {{ill|Yingtian Gate|en|Yingtian Gate|WD=Q11491257|lt=Ōtenmon Gate}} is set ablaze. {{ill|Tomo no Yoshio|en|Tomo no Yoshio|WD=Q7820079|lt=Ban no Yoshio}} , a descendant of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}} , is exiled as the arsonist, marking the decline of the clan’s political influence.
* {{ill|Eiwa (Japan)|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa (Japan)|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 4 / {{ill|Tenju|en|Tenju|WD=Q1145023|lt=Tenju (Japan)}} 4 (Julian {{ill|1378|en|1378|WD=Q6306|lt=1378}} {{ill|April 7|en|April 7|WD=Q2505|lt=April 7}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|en|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|WD=Q248381|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu}} moves the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} to the newly constructed {{ill|Muromachi Palace|ja|室町殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Palace|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} (Hana no Gosho) in Kyoto.
* {{ill|Daoguang Emperor|ja|道光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daoguang Emperor|1=Daoguang Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1133186}} 19 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1839|en|1839|WD=Q7616|lt=1839}} {{ill|April 23|en|April 23|WD=Q2527|lt=April 23}} ) – Qing officials arrive in {{ill|Guangdong|ja|広東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangdong|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広東省|1=Guangdong|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15175}} to enforce a crackdown on {{ill|opium|ja|阿片|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/opium|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヘン|1=opium|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q46452}} , leading to large-scale confiscation and destruction.
* {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}} {{ill|April 21|en|April 21|WD=Q2525|lt=April 21}} ) – The {{ill|Tokyo City|en|Tokyo City|WD=Q1207735|lt=Tokyo City}} abolishes the {{ill|machinushi|ja|町名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/machinushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 庄屋|1=shōya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095960}} system, dismissing 238 town chiefs.
* Meiji 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|April 17|en|April 17|WD=Q2520|lt=April 17}} ) – The {{ill|Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|en|Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|WD=Q11080185|lt=Ministry of Education (Japan)}} establishes a museum department (now the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}} ) and hosts the first {{ill|exposition|ja|博覧会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/exposition|1=hakurankai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112298281}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|en|Yushima Seidō|WD=Q1760591|lt=Yushima Seidō}} 's Taiseiden ({{ill|Yushima Seidō Exposition|en|Yushima Seidō Exposition|WD=Q107150759|lt=Yushima Seidō Exposition}} ).
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chōshō|en|Chōshō|WD=Q1194264|lt=Chōshō (era)}} 3 (Julian {{ill|1134|en|1134|WD=Q19590|lt=1134}} {{ill|April 6|en|April 6|WD=Q2506|lt=April 6}} ) – {{ill|Yasuda Yoshisada|en|Yasuda Yoshisada|WD=Q11451109|lt=Yasuda Yoshisada}} , Japanese {{ill|samurai|ja|武将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=military commander|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545923}} (+ {{ill|1194|en|1194|WD=Q19743|lt=1194}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 5 (Julian {{ill|1536|en|1536|WD=Q6457|lt=1536}} {{ill|March 31|en|March 31|WD=Q2461|lt=March 31}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|en|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|WD=Q447066|lt=Ashikaga Yoshiteru}} , 13th {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} (+ {{ill|1565|en|1565|WD=Q6634|lt=1565}} ).
* {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei (era)}} 5 (Gregorian {{ill|1822|en|1822|WD=Q6983|lt=1822}} {{ill|May 11|en|May 11|WD=Q2558|lt=May 11}} ) – {{ill|Kurimoto Jō'un|en|Kurimoto Jō'un|WD=Q6526509|lt=Kurikoma Jōun}} , {{ill|bakufu|ja|幕臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bakufu|1=bakushin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481849}} official and {{ill|Journalist|en|Journalist|WD=Q1930187|lt=journalist}} (+ {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897}} ).
* {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei (era)}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1848|en|1848|WD=Q7647|lt=1848}} {{ill|April 13|en|April 13|WD=Q2498|lt=April 13}} ) – {{ill|Asano Sōichirō|en|Asano Sōichirō|WD=Q4803533|lt=Asano Sōichirō}} , entrepreneur and founder of the {{ill|Asano zaibatsu|en|Asano zaibatsu|WD=Q4160702|lt=Asano zaibatsu}} (+ {{ill|1930|en|1930|WD=Q18787|lt=1930}} ).
* {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō (era)}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|en|1866|WD=Q7712|lt=1866}} {{ill|April 24|en|April 24|WD=Q2529|lt=April 24}} ) – {{ill|Ishii Kikujirō|en|Ishii Kikujirō|WD=Q129782|lt=Ishii Kikujirō}} , Japanese {{ill|Diplomat|en|Diplomat|WD=Q193391|lt=diplomat}} (+ {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945}} ).
* {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 4 (Gregorian {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} {{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29}} ) – {{ill|Yoshioka Yayoi|ja|吉岡弥生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Yayoi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉岡彌生|1=Yoshioka Yayoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138424}} , physician and founder of {{ill|Tokyo Women's Medical University|en|Tokyo Women's Medical University|WD=Q11524729|lt=Tokyo Women's Medical University}} (+ {{ill|1959|en|1959|WD=Q5302|lt=1959}} ).
== Deaths ==
* Emperor Monmu 4 (Julian {{ill|700|en|700|WD=Q23494|lt=700}} {{ill|April 3|en|April 3|WD=Q2509|lt=April 3}} ) – {{ill|Dōshō|en|Dōshō|WD=Q724005|lt=Dōshō}} , Buddhist monk.
* {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku (era)}} 9 (Intercalary March) (Julian {{ill|790|en|790|WD=Q31147|lt=790}} {{ill|April 28|en|April 28|WD=Q2534|lt=April 28}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|en|Fujiwara no Otomuro|WD=Q2110461|lt=Fujiwara no Otomuro}} , Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|WD=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu}} (* {{ill|760|en|760|WD=Q30552|lt=760}} ).
* {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|WD=Q1193933|lt=Kenkyū (era)}} 2 (Julian {{ill|1191|en|1191|WD=Q19738|lt=1191}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagakata|ja|藤原長方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Nagakata|1=Nagakata Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623620}} , senior court official and author of *An'nagon Nagakata Collection* and *Zenchūki* (* {{ill|1139|en|1139|WD=Q19608|lt=1139}} ).
* {{ill|Tenbun (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 15 (Julian {{ill|1544|en|1544|WD=Q6525|lt=1544}} {{ill|April 10|en|April 10|WD=Q2502|lt=April 10}} ) – {{ill|Ryūzōji Iekane|ja|龍造寺家兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzōji Iekane|1=Ryūzōji Iekane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5368164}} , feudal lord of {{ill|Kyushu|en|Kyushu|WD=Q13987|lt=Kyushu}} (* {{ill|1454|en|1454|WD=Q6476|lt=1454}} ).
* {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei (era)}} 14 (Gregorian {{ill|1637|en|1637|WD=Q6889|lt=1637}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5}} ) – {{ill|Honda Masazumi|en|Honda Masazumi|WD=Q1058809|lt=Honda Masazumi}} , early Edo-period {{ill|Daimyo|en|Daimyo|WD=Q61982|lt=daimyō}} (* {{ill|1565|en|1565|WD=Q6634|lt=1565}} ).
== Commemorations ==
== See Also ==
